clean_story:
Now I will teach you the great sky-bird of loud thunder:

A giant metal-beast, smooth like wet stone, roars like many fires trapped inside. It flies high where sun burns brightest, no wings flap—just a belly full of men, held up by fast-spinning sticks that cut air like sharp flint. When it lands, earth shakes; when it passes, shadow races below like fleeing herd. Touch its skin—cold as deep-water rock, but hums like angry hornets’ nest. If you see this beast swallow people, then spit them far away, run—it is not alive, but moves faster than any eagle or storm.
